title: centaurium erythraea var. rumelica
intro: Centaurium erythraea var. rumelica, commonly known as mountain centaury, is a rare and endemic plant species found in the Balkan Peninsula. It belongs to the Gentianaceae family and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving symptoms of respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin conditions. The mountain centaury is typically used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use and availability may be limited due to its rarity.
